summary: Agent1 recommends using a mixture of potassium oxalate and sodium fluoride as an anticoagulant when sending blood samples for glucose tests due to its effectiveness in preserving glucose stability compared to other options like heparin alone. This choice ensures more reliable results by preventing both short-term degradation from glycolytic activity and potential inaccuracies introduced through extended handling or transportation delays. To summarize further if needed but keeping within constraints, this method offers robust protection against changes affecting test outcomes crucially important clinically speaking here. The primary benefit lies not only in immediate accuracy post-collection but also ensuring data reliability throughout transit until analysis can occur reliably later without compromising patient care
